title: Loop Spec
description: Declarative definition of one loop-engineering loop. Each component names a Python implementation of a loopeng contract via a dotted import path ('uses') plus its config ('with'). A runtime loads this spec, instantiates the components, and drives find -> act -> verify -> remember -> repeat -> stop.

name.description: Stable identifier for this loop; used as the goal_id in state.

goal.description: A loopeng.Goal implementation. Its is_satisfied() is the GOAL_MET stop condition.

work_source.description: A loopeng.WorkSource implementation. find() discovers outstanding work items.

actor.description: A loopeng.Actor implementation. act() performs one work item.

sensors.description: One or more loopeng.Sensor implementations. ALL must pass for an action to count as progress.

stop.description: One or more loopeng.StopCondition implementations. The loop stops on the first that fires. At least one bound is required; an unbounded loop is rejected.

state_store.description: A loopeng.StateStore implementation. Defaults to a runtime-provided JSON file store under state/ if omitted.

isolation.description: How parallel work units are isolated from each other.

budget.description: Hard ceilings. Reaching one stops the loop with the matching StopReason.

uses.description: Dotted import path to the implementing class, e.g. 'myloops.sensors.PytestSensor'.

with.description: Keyword arguments passed to the implementation's constructor.
